unmapped.human_action: Watch for: ceramics studio closures in urban markets, launch of outcome-focused or results-first ceramics curriculum products, re-enrollment rate disclosures from studio chains or co-op networks. A second independent source describing the same expectation mismatch would materially upgrade confidence.
unmapped.machine_action: Monitor RSS feeds and social for ceramics or pottery studio closures, new beginner-format studio launches, editorial pieces describing dropout or dissatisfaction with craft class instruction, and any studio operators discussing curriculum redesign.
unmapped.methodology_footer: Single RSS source surfaced by editorial signal monitor. Confidence held at low pending second independent signal. Not a repeat of the June 8 miniatures issue — that piece concerned beginner supply infrastructure for a TV-driven entry cohort; this concerns retention economics and curriculum design inside a mature studio category.
unmapped.uncertainty: The dropout described may be isolated to poor instruction at a single studio rather than structural across the category. The ceramics studio market lacks centralized enrollment or retention data, making the thesis difficult to verify quickly. Adjacent craft-hobby onboarding signals (miniatures, June 8) concern market entry, not retention — this proposal is differentiated by focusing on the curriculum-expectation gap inside an already-adopted hobby.

original_issue.falsification: Strong re-enrollment data at existing ceramics studios; emergence of scaled outcome-first studio formats already addressing the mismatch; no corroborating dropout signals from a second independent source within 60 days.
